person.name: WIlliam VAUX Esq of Bottisham and Northampton

nameForms.fullText: WIlliam VAUX Esq of Bottisham and Northampton

memoryArtifacts.description:
The Visitation of Oxfordshire (1566, 1574, 1634) (p. 178), gives arms for Eleanor Vaux, wife of Thomas Giffard, i.e., Chequy argent and gules.  The Visitation of Buckinghamshire, 1634 (p. 166) shows arms for Gifford of Middle Claydon (descendants of Eleanor Vaux) that include quarterings for Gifford, Winslow and Vaux, among others.  The arms shown for Vaux are Chequy argent and gules on a chevron azure three roses or, very similar to that for Vaux of Harrowden (Chequy or and gules on a chevron azure three roses or).  Eleanor's father was ancestor of Vaux of Harrowden.
------------------------------
Vaux of Harrowden. Checky or and gules a cheveron azure with three roses or thereon.

http://www.british-history.ac.uk/vch/bucks/vol4/pp462-466 - 1 Oct 2016

zh.lifeSummary: 當WIlliam VAUX Esq of Bottisham and Northampton於1374年出生在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾格亞特 哈洛登時，他的父親William Vaux-1歲，母親Eleanor Drakelowe0歲。他於大約1398年與Eleanor Drakelowe在英國賀福爾理查茲 卡素結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1460年12月10日在英國諾薩普頓史爾格亞特 哈洛登享年86歲，埋葬在英國越可爾可利亞斯比。

nameMeanings.name: VAUX
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of various places in northern France called Vaux, from the Old French plural of val ‘valley’. Compare English Vause and French Devaux .

History: Richard Vaux (1749–90) emigrated from Sussex, England, to Philadelphia, PA, in early youth. Calvert Vaux, landscape architect, emigrated from London, England, to America in 1850 and designed several major US parks, including Central Park in New York City (with Frederick Olmstead). — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its variant Veaux and altered form Devoe ) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Vause
meanings: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Vaux (see Vaux ), a common French placename, singular and plural of val ‘valley’, the plural form being Latinized in surnames as de Vallibus. Norman lords with this surname are recorded in 12th- and early 13th-century records in Cumberland (Irthington), Devon (Farwood, in Colyton), East Yorkshire (Warter), Essex (Belchamp), and Leicestershire (Belvoir). Compare Vale .

nickname perhaps from Middle English fals, faus, vaus (Old French fals, faus, Old English fals) ‘deceitful, disloyal, dishonest, mendacious’, but it is more likely the name arises from 1, with the common Anglo-Norman French substitution of le for de in their surname.

nameMeanings.name: WIlliam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When WIlliam VAUX Esq of Bottisham and Northampton was born in 1374, in Great Harrowden, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, William Vaux, was -1 and his mother, Eleanor Drakelowe, was 0. He married Eleanor Drakelowe about 1398, in Richards Castle, Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 10 December 1460, in Great Harrowden, Northamptonshire, England, at the age of 86, and was buried in Cleasby, Yorkshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: WIlliam VAUX Esq of Bottisham and Northampton(이)가 1374년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 그리트 해로우든에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Vaux(은)는 -1세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor Drakelowe(은)는 0세였다. 그는 대략 1398년 영국 허퍼드실르 리처스 카스틀에서 Eleanor Drakelowe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1460년 12월 10일 영국 노르섬프토실르 그리트 해로우든에서 86세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 클리스비에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда WIlliam VAUX Esq of Bottisham and Northampton родился в 1374 г. в местности грит харроуден, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William Vaux было -1 год, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Drakelowe, 0. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor Drakelowe около 1398 г. в местности ричардс кастл, хирефордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 10 декабря 1460 г. в местности грит харроуден, нортамптоншир, Англия в возрасте 86 лет и был похоронен в местности клисби, еркшир, Англия.
